body:
MORNING INTEL — 2026-07-11 (snapshot 2026-07-11_1)
Regime data: VIX 15.03 | HY OAS 270 | Breadth 90.9%
AMZN: NO_TRADE — IV Rank below minimum or missing (G2_iv_rank: observed None threshold >= 25.0)
NVDA: NO_TRADE — IV Rank below minimum or missing (G2_iv_rank: observed None threshold >= 25.0)
SPY: NO_TRADE — IV Rank below minimum or missing (G2_iv_rank: observed None threshold >= 25.0)
Portfolio risk after fills: pending Artha confirmation
Discipline: 30d system PnL $0 | override PnL $0
Data quality: all sources OK

structured.takeaway: The current regime shows a moderately elevated VIX at 15.03 with a steep contango between the 9-day (11.15) and 3-month (18.57) implying short-term calm but longer-term uncertainty, while HY OAS remains elevated at 270 bps, signaling credit stress. SPY at $754.95 with subdued IV of 19.05% and negative sentiment (-1) is vulnerable to a leveraged ETF-driven selloff, as highlighted by the $200 billion in leveraged ETFs that could exacerbate downside pressure. NVDA at $210.96 with a high IV of 45.27% and positive sentiment (+1) is buoyed by strong AI sector momentum, notably SK Hynix’s 14% jump on its US debut and bullish commentary from Jim Cramer, making NVDA the preferred long in semiconductors. AMZN at $245.34 with neutral sentiment and no fresh catalysts is likely to trade sideways ahead of the heavy bank earnings slate starting July 14, which could set broader market tone. The key near-term catalyst is the upcoming bank earnings, which will test market stability amid rising rates (US10Y at 4.54%) and elevated credit spreads; any signs of weakness here could trigger a broader selloff amplified by leveraged ETF positioning. System is in warmup with IV rank gating inactive, so expect some noise but no full signal deployment yet.

SPY.sentiment_driver: Concerns over leveraged ETFs amplifying market selloff
SPY.gate_failure_detail: IV Rank below minimum or missing

NVDA.sentiment_driver: SK Hynix shares jump on AI wave
NVDA.gate_failure_detail: IV Rank below minimum or missing

AMZN.gate_failure_detail: IV Rank below minimum or missing

news.title: Analyst Reveals How $200 Billion in Leveraged ETFs Could Amplify the Next Market Selloff
SK Hynix shares jump 14% in debut on US stock market riding on AI wave
Jim Cramer Says NVIDIA Is the Most Proprietary Chip Company in History, and the Market Is Getting Its Valuation Wrong

narratives.this_week: Bank earnings dominate this week with major reports from BAC, WFC, JPM, and GS expected on July 14, followed by MS on July 15. Market consensus anticipates stable financial results with moderate growth and cautious outlooks, suggesting limited volatility in financials and a neutral impact on SPY. Investors should watch for any surprises in loan growth or margin trends that could ripple through broader equity sentiment, while NVDA and AMZN remain largely unaffected by this sector-specific focus.
narratives.next_week: Next week, attention shifts to Communication Services and Industrials with GOOGL and TSLA earnings on July 22, alongside key reports from RTX, HON, and materials names like NEM and FCX. GOOGL’s ad growth and AI investments will be critical for gauging tech sector momentum, potentially providing a bullish catalyst for NVDA given AI’s centrality. TSLA’s cautious growth outlook may temper consumer discretionary enthusiasm, while industrials and materials results will offer clues on economic resilience, influencing SPY’s cyclical exposure.
narratives.next_month: Looking further ahead, the earnings calendar intensifies with heavyweight Technology and Consumer Discretionary names such as MSFT, AMZN, AAPL, and META reporting between July 29-30, coinciding with the FOMC meeting and July PCE data. MSFT and AMZN’s cloud and AI investment narratives remain key drivers for tech sector leadership and NVDA’s outlook, while the Fed’s likely hold on rates and inflation data will set the tone for risk appetite across equities. Energy names CVX and XOM also report, expected to confirm stable demand, supporting defensive sectors within SPY amid macro uncertainty.
